title: Improving Frontend Performance
questions: How do you diagnose frontend performance problems?
Describe a time you improved a slow web application.
Which metrics do you use to measure frontend performance?
answer: I begin by measuring performance with browser profiling tools and metrics such as Largest Contentful Paint, Interaction to Next Paint, and Cumulative Layout Shift. In one project, I identified an oversized JavaScript bundle and unnecessary component re-renders as the main bottlenecks. I introduced route-based code splitting, lazy-loaded non-critical components, optimized images, and memoized expensive calculations. These changes reduced the initial bundle size and improved page responsiveness. I then compared the results against the original performance baseline to verify the improvement.

title: Debugging a Difficult Production Issue
questions: Tell me about a difficult bug you resolved.
How do you investigate production issues?
answer: I first reproduce the issue when possible and collect evidence from logs, monitoring data, recent deployments, and user reports. For a production issue involving intermittent duplicate requests, I traced request identifiers across the frontend and backend and discovered that a retry mechanism was running alongside a manual fallback. I removed the conflicting behavior, added idempotency protection on the server, and introduced regression tests and monitoring. This fixed the immediate problem and reduced the risk of similar failures.
